title: Warm Pillowy Dutch Coconut Pilaf
description: A funky and pillowy Dutch dish featuring coconut flakes, perfect for a date night, and is plant-based friendly. This dish is made with a mortar-pestle-pound method for a unique texture.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
Add the basmati rice and stir to coat the rice with the oil and mix with the onion and garlic.
Add the water, salt, and black pepper, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
While the rice is cooking, use a mortar and pestle to pound the dates into a smooth paste.
Fluff the cooked rice with a fork and stir in the date paste and coconut flakes.
Serve the pilaf warm, garnished with additional coconut flakes if desired.
